headline: Cross-account file-takeover closure, an unauthenticated ZIP-download DoS fix, and admin-API responses that no longer leak per-file keys or bundle secrets
summary: An attacker can no longer acquire another account's anonymously-uploaded files by pointing their own email at the victim's address — an email change now requires proof of control of the new address, and anonymous uploads are reassigned to an account only once that account's email is verified. The unauthenticated bundle ZIP-download endpoints are rate-limited and memory-bounded, the admin file and bundle list APIs no longer return per-file encryption keys, storage paths, bundle password hashes, finalize tokens, or recipient addresses, and a set of access-control, lockout, quota, and webhook issues are fixed.

items.title: Cross-account anonymous-upload takeover is closed
items.body: A signed-in user could acquire another person's anonymously-uploaded files by changing their own account email to the victim's address: the dashboard reassigned every anonymous upload whose recorded uploader address equalled the account's email, with no proof the account controlled that address. Email equality is no longer treated as proof of ownership — anonymous uploads are reassigned to an account only when that account's email is verified, and a fresh or unverified address never triggers a claim. An email change to an address that holds pending anonymous uploads is refused outright.

items.title: Email changes require proof of control of the new address
items.body: Changing an account's email now requires verifying the new address before it grants any privilege, mirroring registration: the change is staged behind a verification round-trip rather than taking effect immediately on the password-confirmed request alone. This closes the path that let an unverified address be used to attribute mail, audit entries, or ownership.

items.title: Unauthenticated bundle ZIP downloads are rate-limited and memory-bounded
items.body: The public full-bundle and folder ZIP-download endpoints buffered every file into memory with no throttle, so an anonymous caller could exhaust server memory by requesting large archives in a loop. Both endpoints are now rate-limited per client and cap the bytes a single archive build holds at once.

items.title: Admin file and bundle APIs no longer leak secrets
items.body: The admin file-list response is now projected to an explicit allowlist of interface fields, so it no longer ships per-file encryption keys, storage paths, or checksums to the browser. The admin bundle-list response likewise no longer exposes bundle password hashes, finalize-token hashes, or the email-gate recipient list. The allowlist is positive, so a future sealed column cannot silently re-leak.

items.title: Stash-password unlock gains the persistent subnet-keyed lockout
items.body: The public stash-password unlock route now shares the same restart-surviving, subnet-keyed exponential-backoff lockout the bundle-password unlock already enforced, lifted into one module so the two routes can't drift. An attacker rotating addresses within a subnet can no longer reset the escalating backoff.

items.title: Stash unlock state is keyed by a stable identifier
items.body: A stash's unlocked session state is now keyed by the stash's stable internal identifier instead of its human-chosen, reusable slug, so a freed-and-reused slug can no longer carry a previous stash's unlock onto a new protected stash.

items.title: Email-gated access is re-checked against the current allow-list
items.body: An email-gated bundle or stash now re-validates the session's verified address against the resource's current allow-list on every request, so removing an address from the gate revokes its access immediately rather than leaving an established session in place.

items.title: Login no longer reveals account existence or lockout state
items.body: A login against a locked account now returns the same uniform response as a non-existent address or a wrong password, removing an oracle that distinguished registered, locked, and unknown accounts. The lockout is still fully enforced.

items.title: A toggled-off webhook stops delivering immediately
items.body: An in-flight or retrying webhook delivery is now suppressed once the operator toggles the hook off, instead of completing to the just-disabled endpoint; the active state is re-checked at delivery time.

items.title: Upload quotas are enforced on the post-write total
items.body: The per-IP byte quota is now reserved the instant its check passes, so concurrent in-flight uploads see each other's debits and can't all clear a stale pre-write total and overshoot the cap; a failed upload releases its reservation.

items.title: Sync enrollment uses an indexed, constant-time code lookup
items.body: Validating a sync enrollment code now uses the indexed derived-hash lookup and a constant-time comparison instead of loading every pending code and matching with plain equality.

items.title: Stash counters are decremented on last-file auto-cleanup
items.body: When a bundle is auto-removed because its last file was deleted, the owning stash's bundle and byte counters are now decremented, matching the other bundle-removal paths.
